zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Beaumont Knight於1514年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾時，他的父親Sir John John Galmyngton Roger Boys John Beaumount Thomas de Wyllyngton William Brythlegh John Poulet Thomas de Wyllyngton John Beaumont19歲，母親Jane Beaumont15歲。他於1527年2月26日與Katherine Gascoigne de Neville在英國英格蘭越可爾利福瑟其結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1589年在他的家鄉享年75歲。

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Beaumont Knight was born in 1514, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John John Galmyngton Roger Boys John Beaumount Thomas de Wyllyngton William Brythlegh John Poulet Thomas de Wyllyngton John Beaumont, was 19 and his mother, Jane Beaumont, was 15. He married Katherine Gascoigne de Neville on 26 February 1527, in Liversedge, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1589, in his hometown, at the age of 75.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Beaumont Knight est né en 1514, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John John Galmyngton Roger Boys John Beaumount Thomas de Wyllyngton William Brythlegh John Poulet Thomas de Wyllyngton John Beaumont, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Jane Beaumont, avait 15 ans. Il a épousé Katherine Gascoigne de Neville le 26 février 1527, à Liversedge, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1589, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 75 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Beaumont Knight(이)가 1514년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John John Galmyngton Roger Boys John Beaumount Thomas de Wyllyngton William Brythlegh John Poulet Thomas de Wyllyngton John Beaumont(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane Beaumont(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1527년 2월 26일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 라이버세지에서 Katherine Gascoigne de Neville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1589년 그의 고향에서 75세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Beaumont Knight родился в 1514 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir John John Galmyngton Roger Boys John Beaumount Thomas de Wyllyngton William Brythlegh John Poulet Thomas de Wyllyngton John Beaumont было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Beaumont, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Katherine Gascoigne de Neville 26 февраля 1527 г. в местности ливирседж, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1589 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 75 лет.
